tested_outcome: Production-ready first pass. Bill-holder hero landed perfectly, $842.64 on bill sold the angle harder than headline could, brand logos rendered correctly.

input_context.headline: DUCTED A/C THAT PAYS FOR ITSELF
input_context.visual_hero_choice: Person holding electricity bill (alternative to default house diagram)
input_context.value_stack: Smart Zoning Technology | Heat or Cool Just the Rooms You Use | 5-Year Workmanship Guarantee | $0 Upfront, Interest-Free Finance Available

assets_attached.instruction: Match style/density/feel, do NOT copy headline/hero/colours

composition_choices_that_worked: Made the bill amount ($842.64) prominently readable — the dollar amount sells the angle harder than the headline does
Set hero photo as middle-aged Australian homeowner, mildly frustrated/concerned (not cartoonish, not over-posed)
Specified 'natural daytime lighting, relatable, not too polished, not too gritty' to land the right tone
Pill-shaped price panel with three vertical lines: small 'DUCTED A/C INSTALLED FOR' / oversized $54 / small 'PER WEEK'
Brand strip at very bottom in native colours, evenly spaced
Did NOT specify colour palette — left to AI ('avoid aggressive reds, this is savings not pain')
Reference attachment did 80% of the heavy lifting on layout/density
full_prompt_used:
Use the attached image as a strong style and layout reference. Match its visual energy, density, and direct-response feel — but DO NOT copy its headline, hero visual, or specific colours. Generate fresh ones per the brief below.

Create a square 1080×1080 social media ad for a residential ducted air conditioning installer in Brisbane, Australia.

MOOD & STYLE
Calm, trustworthy, benefit-led. Choose a fresh modern colour palette of your own — avoid aggressive reds, this is a savings-focused ad, not a pain/urgency ad. Pick clean, modern typography. All text must be high-contrast and readable at thumbnail size. Clean, uncluttered, professional. Don't make it look like a stock template.

LAYOUT (top to bottom)

1. HEADLINE — top third
Oversized bold uppercase text reading exactly: "DUCTED A/C THAT PAYS FOR ITSELF"
Split across two lines if needed. Dominant element of the top section.

2. HERO VISUAL — middle, roughly 45% of canvas height
A photo-real middle-aged Australian homeowner standing in a modern home interior, holding up an electricity bill in front of them with a mildly frustrated/concerned expression. The bill should look real and readable but not show specific identifying details — show large dollar amounts on it. Natural daytime lighting. Relatable, not too polished, not too gritty.

3. PRICE PILL — just below the hero
A horizontal pill-shaped panel containing three lines of text, vertically stacked:
   - Small uppercase caps: "DUCTED A/C INSTALLED FOR"
   - Oversized bold dominant: "$54"
   - Small uppercase caps: "PER WEEK"

4. VALUE STACK — right of (or beneath) the price pill
Four tick-bullet items, evenly spaced:
   ✓ Smart Zoning Technology
   ✓ Heat or Cool Just the Rooms You Use
   ✓ 5-Year Workmanship Guarantee
   ✓ $0 Upfront, Interest-Free Finance Available

5. CTA BUTTON — bottom-left
Rounded pill button reading: "GET YOUR FREE QUOTE"
Clean uppercase. High-contrast colour that pops against the background.

6. DISCOUNT BADGE — top-right or bottom-right corner
Circular burst badge reading: "30% OFF"
Bold contrasting colour.

7. BRAND STRIP — very bottom edge
Thin horizontal strip with three brand logos evenly spaced and rendered in their native brand colours: Daikin, Mitsubishi Heavy Industries, Samsung. Use the real brand logos.

HARD RULES
- 1080×1080 aspect ratio (square), nothing else
- All text must be readable at thumbnail size on a phone
- Maximum 3 brand logos
- Do NOT add "0% interest" anywhere — interest-free finance is fine
- Do NOT add a fixed-price installation amount — only the $54/week price shown
- Do NOT add any people beyond the bill-holder
- Do NOT add review badges or star ratings to this ad
lessons_for_layer_2: When the visual hero is a 'person holding X' shot, brief the photo with concrete details — age, ethnicity, expression, location, lighting — so the AI doesn't default to generic stock-look
Make the prop (bill, key object) PROMINENT and READABLE — that's where 50% of the persuasion lives
The price pill structure 'Small caps / oversized number / small caps' is a reliable hierarchy pattern — reuse for any per-week price ad
Brand logos at the bottom in native colours is a trust signal — never tint, never invert
